public long smallestNumber(long num) {

    if (num == 0) return 0;

    boolean isNegative = num < 0;

    // get the absolute value as a string

    String s = Long.toString(Math.abs(num));

    char[] digits = s.toCharArray();

    if (isNegative) {

        // sort descending

        Arrays.sort(digits, (a, b) -> b - a);

    } else {

        // sort ascending

        Arrays.sort(digits);

    }

    // process digits

    if (isNegative) {

        // combine digits into a string, then parse to long, then multiply by -1

        return -1 * Long.parseLong(new String(digits));

    } else {

        // check if first digit is 0

        if (digits[0] == '0') {

            // find first non-zero

            int firstNonZero = 0;

            while (firstNonZero < digits.length && digits[firstNonZero] == '0') {

                firstNonZero++;

            if (firstNonZero < digits.length) {

                // swap with 0th position

                char temp = digits[0];

                digits[0] = digits[firstNonZero];

                digits[firstNonZero] = temp;

            }

        }

        return Long.parseLong(new String(digits));

    }

}

public long smallestNumber(long num) {
    if (num == 0) return 0;
    boolean isNegative = num < 0;
    String s = Long.toString(Math.abs(num));
    char[] digits = s.toCharArray();

            digits[i] = digits[digits.length - 1 - i];
            digits[digits.length - 1 - i] = temp;

    }
    if (isNegative) {
        return -Long.parseLong(new String(digits));

                digits[0] = digits[firstNonZero];
                digits[firstNonZero] = temp;
            }
        }
        return Long.parseLong(new String(digits));
    }
}

class Solution {
    public long smallestNumber(long num) {
        if (num == 0) return 0;
        boolean isNegative = num < 0;
        String s = Long.toString(Math.abs(num));
        char[] digits = s.toCharArray();
        if (isNegative) {
            Arrays.sort(digits);
            for (int i = 0; i < digits.length / 2; i++) {
                char temp = digits[i];
                digits[i] = digits[digits.length - 1 - i];
                digits[digits.length - 1 - i] = temp;
            }
        } else {
            Arrays.sort(digits);
        }
        if (isNegative) {
            return -Long.parseLong(new String(digits));
        } else {
            if (digits[0] == '0') {
                int firstNonZero = 0;
                while (firstNonZero < digits.length && digits[firstNonZero] == '0') {
                    firstNonZero++;
                }
                if (firstNonZero < digits.length) {
                    char temp = digits[0];
                    digits[0] = digits[firstNonZero];
                    digits[firstNonZero] = temp;
                }
            }
            return Long.parseLong(new String(digits));
        }
    }
}

solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public long smallestNumber(long num) {
        if (num == 0) return 0;
        boolean isNegative = num < 0;
        String s = Long.toString(Math.abs(num));
        char[] digits = s.toCharArray();
        if (isNegative) {
            java.util.Arrays.sort(digits);
            for (int i = 0; i < digits.length / 2; i++) {
                char temp = digits[i];
                digits[i] = digits[digits.length - 1 - i];
                digits[digits.length - 1 - i] = temp;
            }
        } else {
            java.util.Arrays.sort(digits);
        }
        if (isNegative) {
            return -Long.parseLong(new String(digits));
        } else {
            if (digits[0] == '0') {
                int firstNonZero = 0;
                while (firstNonZero < digits.length && digits[firstNonZero] == '0') {
                    firstNonZero++;
                }
                if (firstNonZero < digits.length) {
                    char temp = digits[0];
                    digits[0] = digits[firstNonZero];
                    digits[firstNonZero] = temp;
                }
            }
            return Long.parseLong(new String(digits));
        }
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The algorithm rearranges digits by sorting them in ascending order for positive numbers and descending order for negative numbers. For positive numbers, leading zeros are handled by swapping the first non-zero digit to the front. Sorting dominates the time complexity, making it O(n log n). Space is O(n) for storing digits. The approach is efficient and handles edge cases like leading zeros correctly.
